title: Oreoseris henryi
intro: Oreoseris henryi, also known as Chinese silkweed, is a species of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae. It has been traditionally used in Chinese medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, headache, and rheumatism, due to its anti-inflammatory and antipyretic properties. The bioactive constituents of Oreoseris henryi include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal effects. In traditional Chinese medicine, Oreoseris henryi is often prepared as a decoction or a tincture, and is used to treat conditions such as coughs, colds, and sore throats.
taxonomy: Oreoseris henryi is classified within the family Asteraceae, commonly known as the daisy or sunflower family. This classification places Oreoseris henryi alongside other related plants such as daisies, sunflowers, and zinnias. The genus Oreoseris is a small group of flowering plants native to Asia. Oreoseris henryi is a specific species within this genus and is native to the Himalayan region. The family Asteraceae is a large and diverse group of flowering plants, with over 23,000 known species.
